description_long: This object is a single cigarette, featuring a cylindrical shape with a tan-colored filter at one end and a white paper-wrapped body. The opposite end of the filter is darkened, showing signs of charring and ash, indicating it has been partially smoked. The filter is smooth and slightly rounded, while the body transitions from clean white to a burnt, ashy tip. The cigarette is compact and lightweight, typical of those found discarded on urban sidewalks or held between fingers. No visible branding or text is present on the exterior. The surface finish is mostly matte, with the ashy end appearing rougher and more irregular.
description: A partially smoked cigarette with a tan filter, white paper body, and a dark, ashy tip. The object is compact, cylindrical, and shows clear burn marks at one end.
description_view_0: The cigarette is viewed from the ashy, burnt tip, showing the darkened, rough texture and a slight taper.
description_view_1: A side view reveals the full length, with a tan filter, white body, and a clear transition to the burnt, ashy end.
description_view_2: The filter end is prominent, displaying its smooth, tan surface and rounded edge.
description_view_3: Another side view, similar to view 1, but from the opposite side, again showing the filter, white body, and burnt tip.
attribute_burn_mark: The tip of the cigarette is visibly charred and darkened, with clear burn marks and discoloration.
